api_workflows.name: TriggerTriageAgent
api_workflows.description: Called when a case enters the Triage stage. Sends ticket data to the triage agent and updates case fields.
actions_after: If flags contains 'legal_threat' → create Legal Review action

api_workflows.name: TriggerContextAgent
api_workflows.description: Called when a case enters the Enrichment stage. Pulls CRM data and generates context brief.
actions_after: If customer_tier is VIP → set priority to Critical

api_workflows.name: TriggerResolutionAgent
api_workflows.description: Called when a case enters the Resolution stage. Drafts response and recommends actions.
actions_after: If requires_human_approval → create Action Center task
If NOT requires_human_approval → auto-transition to Satisfaction

api_workflows.name: TriggerSatisfactionAgent
api_workflows.description: Called after resolution is approved. Analyzes satisfaction and predicts churn.
api_workflows.trigger: Case stage transition → Satisfaction (after human approval or auto-resolve)
actions_after: If action == 're_escalate' → transition back to Resolution

deployment_notes: 1. Deploy the Python orchestrator (orchestrator.py) to a server accessible from UiPath Cloud
2. In UiPath Automation Cloud → Integration Service → add HTTP connector
3. Base URL: your deployed orchestrator URL (e.g., https://your-app.azurewebsites.net)
4. Create API Workflows in UiPath Studio Web that call these endpoints
5. Connect each API Workflow to the corresponding Maestro Case stage transition
6. Test end-to-end with a sample ticket
